posts.text: So, we can probably infer this vague idea of a jailbreak is simply a lever for Hegseth and Trump to put pressure on Anthropic, right?

Damnit, dog is not there. I step away for one quiet moment and the US gov forces 

Anthropic to drop the hammer and cut off access to Fable to non US citizens, based on a purported jailbreak. Though Anthropic went the equanimity route and just turned it off for everyone. Thought the prospect of somehow identifying US citizens was probably also unrealistic

Here's a good example of what Fable 5 can do. This is Ultracode effort, on a refactor on Rackula. In ~55min, across 44 agents:
- 6 stacked PRs pushed, ready for review
- 11 new issues identified for follow up

It used 3.87M tokens to do this.

I've hit Fable's safety filter a few times in Claude Code, it seems quite sensitive. No idea what tripped it in this scenario: this is a code review of storage modules in Rackula.

It also just occurred to me that because when you switch models in a conversation the convo history has to be re-read into context (a cache bust), that when it downgrades from Fable to Opus you take that token usage hit. It's a nasty tripwire that I would really like to understand how to avoid
